channel.title: Posts on Rodrigo Perez del Pulgar Almodovar
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Rodrigo Perez del Pulgar Almodovar

item.title: DDJ100 Mixing Console
item.content:
After quite some time without working on any personal projects, I came across a heavily damaged Pioneer DDJ-1000 controller for a very good price, so I decided to buy it.

The jog wheels were broken, the four volume faders were snapped, and several buttons were damaged. I ordered the replacement parts and, after soldering and replacing quite a few components, it works perfectly again.

I also had to design the caps for almost all the knobs and 3D-print them. Here are some photos I took during the process.

Time to DJ!

item.title: Hiatus
item.description: A long break from personal projects
item.content:
This past year has been quite a long one. I haven’t worked on many personal projects during this time because most of my effort has been focused on finishing my Bachelor’s Thesis.

The project required a significant amount of time, both for the research and the implementation, so many of the ideas and small projects I had in mind had to be put on hold for a while.

Now that it is finally finished, I hope to slowly get back to building things again and sharing them here.

item.title: Useful Pages
item.description: Who knows, maybe you will find your next favorite website here...
item.content:
Websites I use regularly

  • Thingiverse: Huge collection of printable 3D models.
  • Gulag Cleaner: Removes abusive ads from Wuolah PDFs.
  • F-Secure Identity Theft Checker: Checks if your data appears in a data breach.
  • OpenSubtitles: Useful if you need subtitles.
  • OnShape: The CAD software I use.
  • MailTicking: Temporary Gmail accounts for sketchy websites.

Essential Chrome extensions (my main browser)

  • Bypass Paywalls Chrome Clean: Bypasses abusive paywalls on some sites.
  • Video Speed Pro: Controls playback speed on web players.
  • uBlock Origin: Excellent ad blocker and anti-tracking extension.

item.title: Range Hood Repair
item.description: 3D design of replacement parts to repair a glass panel support.
item.content:
A few days ago, the glass panel support of my home range hood broke, and my mother asked me to design a 3D-printed replacement part. I use a Creality V3 KE, and it has been great.

After measuring the original part with a caliper and iterating through a couple of prints, I got a strong and accurate fit.

You can find the 3D model on GitHub:

Download the model on GitHub
View on GitHub

item.title: What is BrainMade?
item.content:
The logo shown in all posts and in the About me section indicates that this website was created by a human, not by a generative tool.

Although artificial intelligence is a powerful and useful tool, we strongly believe it should not replace personal creation.

“Human creativity has a unique value that should not be lost in an increasingly automated world.”

If you want to learn more about this initiative, you can click the Brain Made icon.

item.title: Dart Machine Modernization
item.description: Upgrading an old LED display system to OLED.
item.content:
At a lunch with my mother, I met the CEO of a darts federation that owns a large stock of old dart machines. They have been looking for someone to help upgrade the displays, which still rely on 7-segment displays and LEDs from the 80s and 90s.

I said I could help and visited the warehouse with a friend for initial testing.

First impressions

The machines are very vintage. I searched for manuals beforehand but found very little, likely because they were custom-developed for that federation.

Technical description

The main board connects all machine parts and buttons. The display is connected through a 40-pin bus.

  • 17 pins carry information.
  • 3 pins are: one unknown, one ground, and one power.
  • 13 pins vary.
  • 4 pins are constant, all 1s.

Action plan

For reverse engineering, we will try different button combinations and observe how bus bits change. We suspect the processor sends state changes rather than full data, meaning part of the processing happens on the display board.

Next steps

We will start by decoding the bus bit meanings, then analyze the display PCB in detail.

item.title: 3D Models
item.description: Creating printable 3D models, including useful parts like generic batteries and motors.
item.content:
I have created several printable 3D models using the online CAD tool OnShape. I took a free course and learned everything I needed to design practical parts.

I upload to GitHub the models that can be useful, such as generic batteries or motors. In the future, I would like to get a 3D scanner to speed up repair workflows.

Visit the project
View on GitHub

item.title: Failed Project: Robotic Arm
item.description: Design, printing, and control ideas for a robotic arm to polish gemstones.
item.content:
One day I saw a video about gemstone polishing and faceting. Everything was manual, so I thought about building a robotic arm that could facet stones based on a 3D model.

I knew almost nothing about CAD at that point, so I took a short course and started building.

General idea

  1. A robotic arm holding the stone, with three axes plus final rotation.
  2. A rotating abrasive base to remove layers.
  3. A control algorithm for arm movement.

My workflow

  1. Bought servos on AliExpress.
  2. Designed the arm around those servos.
  3. Started coding with Arduino.

Main issue: cheap servos had too much backlash and too little torque, so the system was not precise enough.

The algorithm (I never finished)

The plan was to model the stone as a 3D matrix, compare it with the target faceting model, compute the material to remove, detect planar regions, and translate that into arm movements and cut depths.

Final thought

I still want to implement the surface-detection algorithm one day. It would be a great challenge.

item.title: 3D-Printed Logo and Keychains
item.content:
A colleague of mine is launching a car rental company, and I proposed making custom keychains as a marketing asset. I had already helped with his logo, brand registration, photos, and custom plate frames. Then we came up with this keychain idea.

Design ideas

We settled on two keychain concepts:

  1. A round keychain with the brand initials, LR.
  2. A license-plate keychain to identify each car.

Since the logo has two colors and my printer is single-color, I designed the keychain in separate parts and glued them after printing.

Printing process

  • Logo keychain: I printed a black circular base with grooves for white letters. Due to 3D printing tolerances, I had to run several iterations to get a proper fit.

  • License-plate keychain: This one was trickier because I wanted the same typography as real Spanish plates. I designed the text in Photoshop, converted it to STL with Image to STL, then extruded and adjusted it in OnShape.

Final details

We bought key rings from AliExpress because the cost was much lower and quality was equivalent to local hardware stores.

Next steps

The keychains turned out great. My next idea is to add an NFC sticker to the logo keychain, so customers can tap with their phone and open a landing page with links to the rental site and social media.

item.title: SPO32
item.description: A project that combines multiple daily tools in one device, including a digital clock and Spotify controller.
item.content:
This is the most complex project I have built so far. I wanted to combine several everyday tasks in a single device so I would not have to switch windows constantly.

Implemented features include:

  • A digital clock showing current, maximum, and minimum daily temperature.
  • A Spotify controller with track info, remaining time, and playback control.

Challenges and solutions

The first big challenge was using two LCD screens at once. Their registers conflicted, so both could not be connected directly to the same ESP32. I solved this using a multiplexer.

The ESP32 connects to multiple APIs: weather, time, and Spotify.

Screen switching is done with a button, and music control uses a rotary encoder.

Take a look at the demo video or the GitHub repository.

Watch video

Visit the project
View on GitHub

item.title: STYLO-BOX
item.description: A university project including a jukebox and a custom stylophone built with STM32.
item.content:
This project was developed for the Digital Systems 2 course at my university. The main task was to build a jukebox using an STM32 microcontroller programmed in C.

Extra challenge: custom stylophone

For the optional part, I added a custom stylophone. I used sensors for the keys and added features like octave switching. The implementation was complex because the codebase relied on state machines and register-level programming without using HAL.

Result

Despite the difficulties, the project was a success and I received the Best Hardware Design badge.

You can explore the code on GitHub.

Visit the project
View on GitHub

item.title: HACKATHON - EESTech Challenge 2024 - MINSAIT
item.description: Participation in a university hackathon developing ML models to predict IMDB scores.
item.content:
I had the chance to join a 3-day hackathon at my university, where we developed machine learning models to predict movie ratings on IMDB. During the event, I learned new skills such as image processing and gained deeper understanding of AI models.

The hackathon had two phases

  1. Movie rating prediction

    The first phase focused on predicting a movie score based on its features (actors, category, year, duration, etc.). We were given a dataset of around 10,000 movies and had to train our models with it.

  2. Prediction from movie posters

    The second phase was similar, but using movie posters. Here, image processing became key. My approach was to analyze image pixels and extract the three most frequent colors.

Even though the final results were not top-ranked, it was an excellent learning experience.

The winners from our venue, who reached the European final, showed an impressive level. It was exhausting but also very rewarding.

item.title: AUTO-YT
item.description: An automated system to generate YouTube content using the Google API and scraping.
item.content:
I wanted to explore YouTube monetization by creating an automated channel. Using the Google YouTube API and some scraping techniques, I managed to generate content in minutes without manual editing or content hunting.

How does it work?

To run the system correctly, you need:

  • Instagram account credentials to fetch videos from followed accounts.
  • Access to the Google account of the channel where videos will be uploaded.
  • A developer account for authentication.

The program stores uploaded video IDs in a database. When it looks for new videos, it checks whether they were uploaded before to avoid duplicates.

Available options

  1. Upload a YouTube Short

    The program looks for vertical videos in followed Instagram accounts. Steps:

    • It checks whether the video already exists in the database.
    • If the video is under 1 minute and meets the requirements, it shows a console link for user confirmation.
    • After confirmation, the video is uploaded automatically to YouTube Shorts.

    Tip: adjust the video name and description before uploading.

  2. Create video compilations

    • Lets you select multiple videos to build a compilation.
    • You can choose how many videos to scrape from Instagram and whether to add an intro/outro.
    • This process is slower than Shorts because MoviePy is used for editing and formatting.

Interested in the code?

Visit the project
[GitHub - Under maintenance]

item.title: Software for SAWY
item.description: Automated solutions to generate exercises and questions from educational video content.
item.content:
Working for SAWY, I developed two main tools that significantly improved the previous workflow.

TEXT2QU

TEXT2QU is private software designed to automate puzzle-style programming exercise generation for online courses. It can produce hundreds of questions in seconds.

TEXT2Q

TEXT2Q focuses on automatically generating multiple-choice, true/false, and fill-in-the-gap questions from educational videos.

Both tools were built in Python using Pandas and OpenAI. Generated outputs still require some post-processing, but the time savings compared to manual creation are huge.

item.title: EZCALENDAR
item.description: A Python tool to automatically generate exam schedules in PDF format.
item.content:
Tired of making exam or event schedules by hand?

I built EZCALENDAR in Python to avoid that repetitive task. With this tool, you can generate a printable PDF exam schedule, and it also shows the days remaining until each exam.

How does it work?

  1. Enter exam dates and names.
  2. Press Enter.
  3. Automatically get an A4 PDF with events organized and vertically adjusted based on how many entries you have.

Example generated schedule

If you want to try it, visit the project on GitHub.

Visit the project
View on GitHub

item.title: DJ Mixer Repair
item.content:
I have always loved mixing music, and one of my dreams is to become a DJ. The problem is that good mixers are expensive.

My first mixer was a Traktor. At one party someone spilled a drink on it, and the next day it was basically dead. I disassembled it and kept it for spare parts.

Years later, a friend gave me a damaged Pioneer with several faulty buttons. I tried to repair it, but my desoldering skills were limited, and I damaged several solder pads.

I had to scrape copper traces with a needle and solder replacement parts from the old Traktor. It was messy, but it works.

Main lesson: soldering temperature matters if you do not want to destroy your PCB. I definitely need a better soldering station.

item.title: Art + Robotics Project
item.content:
My mother is a sculptor and wanted to simulate leaf movement using magnets. I helped by designing a circuit where a stepper motor controls a magnet with pseudo-random motion.

At first I considered using an electromagnet, but that would create a regular oscillation frequency. I preferred changing distance and rotation of a permanent magnet for a more natural motion.

A demo video will be added soon.

For the stepper motor, I designed a magnet holder with multiple positions. Even though we currently use two edge positions, the design allows easy changes and adjustments.

item.title: Arduino Projects
item.description: Several Arduino projects including circuit diagrams and practical ideas.
item.content:
I have worked on several Arduino projects, some of which include circuit schematics that can inspire other developers and electronics enthusiasts. Even though the project folder has not been very updated lately, it is still a good source of ideas.

Visit the project
View on GitHub
